description_long: This helmet is modeled after a classical Greek Corinthian design, featuring a smooth, rounded dome that covers the entire head and a prominent, forward-facing crest running from front to back. The front of the helmet has a T-shaped opening for the eyes and mouth, with sharply defined cheek guards that extend downward and inward, providing facial protection while leaving the eyes and mouth exposed. The crest is a solid, flat fin that rises above the helmet and curves slightly backward, evoking the traditional horsehair plume of ancient Greek warriors. The surface is uniformly matte and unadorned, with no visible textures, patterns, or embellishments. The helmet appears to be made of a single solid material, likely metal, and lacks any visible fastenings, straps, or interior padding. Its overall appearance is robust, functional, and historically inspired, suitable for display or costume use in an urban environment.
description: A matte, solid-colored Corinthian-style helmet with a prominent crest and T-shaped facial opening, featuring smooth, unadorned surfaces and pronounced cheek guards.
description_view_0: Front view shows the T-shaped eye and mouth opening, with cheek guards and the central crest visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the full length and curve of the crest, as well as the helmet's rounded dome and cheek guard profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the smooth, rounded back of the helmet and the straight, vertical crest running along the top.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the crest's shape and the helmet's side profile with cheek guard.
